skill.name: Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent (.NET)
skill.description: Azure AI Agents Persistent SDK for .NET. Low-level SDK for creating and managing AI agents with threads, messages, runs, and tools.
skill.summary: Build persistent AI agents with conversation threads, streaming responses, function calling, and file search using Azure AI Agents SDK for .NET.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis found 0 security issues with risk score 0/100. The SKILL.md file contains legitimate Azure SDK usage examples for agent creation, thread management, streaming, and function calling. No security concerns identified.

content.user_title: Build persistent AI agents with Azure AI SDK for .NET
content.value_statement: Developers need to integrate persistent AI agents into .NET applications with proper thread management and streaming. This skill provides complete SDK patterns for agent CRUD, conversation flows, function calling, and file search using Azure AI Agents.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage persistent AI agents with custom instructions and tools
Manage conversation threads with message history and context
Stream agent responses in real-time with incremental updates
Implement function calling with custom tool definitions
Configure file search with vector stores for document retrieval
Integrate Bing Grounding and Azure AI Search for external data
limitations: Requires Azure AI resource with appropriate permissions and endpoint configuration
Preview package versions may have breaking changes between releases
Requires polling or streaming patterns for long-running agent operations
Function execution requires manual implementation of tool output handlers

use_cases.title: Build a Math Tutor Agent
use_cases.description: Create a persistent agent that solves math problems using code interpreter. Users submit questions via thread messages, and the agent executes Python code to compute answers with step-by-step explanations.

use_cases.title: Document Search Assistant
use_cases.description: Implement an agent with vector store file search to answer questions from uploaded documentation. Upload files, create vector stores, and query through natural language conversations.
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise developers creating knowledge base chatbots

use_cases.title: Weather Bot with Function Calling
use_cases.description: Build an agent that calls external weather APIs through function definitions. Define tool schemas, handle RequiresAction responses, and return formatted weather data to users.
use_cases.target_user: Developers integrating custom APIs with AI agents

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a persistent AI agent using Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent SDK. The agent should use gpt-4o-mini model with instructions to act as a helpful assistant. Show authentication with DefaultAzureCredential and basic agent creation code.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Streaming Responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to stream agent responses in real-time using CreateRunStreamingAsync. Include handling for MessageContentUpdate and run status events like RunCreated and RunCompleted.

prompt_templates.title: Add Function Calling to Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an agent with a custom function tool for getting weather data. Define the FunctionToolDefinition with JSON schema parameters, handle RequiresAction status, and submit tool outputs back to the run.

prompt_templates.title: Configure File Search with Vector Store
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up file search capabilities for an agent. Upload a document file, create a vector store with the file ID, configure FileSearchToolResource, and create an agent that can search documents to answer questions.

output_examples.input: Create a math tutor agent with code interpreter enabled
output_examples.output: Agent created successfully with ID 'asst_abc123'. The agent is configured with gpt-4o-mini model and CodeInterpreterTool. Instructions: 'You are a personal math tutor. Write and run code to answer math questions.' Ready to create threads and process user messages.

output_examples.input: Stream a response from the agent for user question about solving 3x + 11 = 14
output_examples.output: Streaming output: 'To solve 3x + 11 = 14, I need to isolate x. First, subtract 11 from both sides: 3x = 3. Then divide by 3: x = 1. Let me verify this with code.' Run completed with final answer showing x equals 1.

best_practices: Always dispose clients using 'using' statements or explicit disposal to release resources
Poll agent runs with 500ms delays between status checks to avoid rate limiting
Delete threads, agents, and vector stores when finished to clean up resources
anti_patterns: Do not skip error handling for RequestFailedException when calling agent APIs
Avoid hardcoding credentials or endpoint URLs directly in application code
Avoid tight polling loops without delays which can trigger rate limits

faq.question: What is the difference between PersistentAgentsClient and Azure.AI.Projects?
faq.answer: PersistentAgentsClient provides low-level direct access to agent operations. Azure.AI.Projects offers a higher-level abstraction that wraps the persistent agents functionality with additional project management features.

faq.question: How do I handle function calling in a streaming run?
faq.answer: Function calling requires polling pattern, not streaming. When run status is RequiresAction, extract the function call details, execute your function, submit tool outputs, then continue polling until completion.

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: The SDK supports DefaultAzureCredential which includes managed identity, Azure CLI credentials, Visual Studio credentials, and service principal authentication via environment variables.

faq.question: How long do threads and messages persist?
faq.answer: Threads and messages persist indefinitely until explicitly deleted. Store thread IDs to resume conversations later. Consider implementing cleanup logic for unused threads to manage costs.

faq.question: Can I use multiple tools with a single agent?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure multiple tools when creating an agent. Pass an array containing CodeInterpreterToolDefinition, FileSearchToolDefinition, FunctionToolDefinition, and other tool types as needed.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for agent operations?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on your Azure AI resource tier. Implement exponential backoff for RequestFailedException with status 429. Default polling interval of 500ms is generally safe for most scenarios.
